goal: Get a pick-and-place demo visibly working for a Tuesday customer call using a prebuilt template.
thoughts: For a Tuesday demo, a prebuilt pick-and-place template IS the right answer -- not IK + grasp control + behavior trees on day 3. If he insists on 'his' box/robot, keep it simple: scripted joint waypoints, no RL policy. The customer cares about the pixels moving, not the provenance.
code:
templates = list_scene_templates(category="manipulation", tags=["pick_place"])
load_scene_template(
    template_name="<PICK_PLACE_PRESET>",
    target_path="/World"
)
# or:
# quick_demo(name="franka_pick_place")
sim_control(action="play")
kb = lookup_knowledge(
    query="Isaac Sim 5.x pick and place template how it works beginner explanation"
)
failure_modes: Pushing him into IK, grasp pose generators and behavior trees
Suggesting Blender-style keyframe animation when the template works out of the box
Refusing to use the preset because his robot is 'different' -- wastes the Tuesday deadline
